normal.name: Normal / Healthy Eye
normal.description: The eye appears healthy with no detectable signs of disease or abnormality in the fundus image. The optic disc, macula, and retinal vessels show normal characteristics.
normal.recommendation: Continue regular annual eye check-ups. Maintain a healthy diet rich in leafy greens and omega-3 fatty acids. Wear UV-protective sunglasses outdoors.

cataract.name: Cataract
cataract.description: A cataract is a clouding of the eye's natural lens, which lies behind the iris and the pupil. It is one of the leading causes of vision impairment worldwide, affecting over 24 million Americans over age 40.
symptoms: Double vision in a single eye
cataract.recommendation: Schedule a consultation with an ophthalmologist. Early cataracts can be managed with updated glasses or contact lenses. Advanced cataracts typically require surgical removal (phacoemulsification), which is a safe and highly effective procedure.

glaucoma.name: Glaucoma
glaucoma.description: Glaucoma is a group of eye conditions that damage the optic nerve, often caused by abnormally high pressure in the eye (intraocular pressure). It is a leading cause of blindness for people over 60.
symptoms: Blurred vision and halos around lights
glaucoma.recommendation: Seek prompt medical attention. Glaucoma is the 'silent thief of sight' — vision loss is irreversible. Treatment options include prescription eye drops, laser treatment (trabeculoplasty), or surgery. Early detection is critical to preserving vision.

diabetic_retinopathy.name: Diabetic Retinopathy
diabetic_retinopathy.description: Diabetic retinopathy is a diabetes complication that affects the eyes, caused by damage to the blood vessels of the light-sensitive tissue at the back of the eye (retina). It is the leading cause of vision loss among working-age adults.
symptoms: Spots or dark strings floating in vision (floaters)
Dark or empty areas in vision
diabetic_retinopathy.recommendation: Immediately consult an ophthalmologist and your diabetes care team. Blood sugar, blood pressure, and cholesterol management are crucial. Treatment includes laser treatment (photocoagulation), anti-VEGF injections, or vitrectomy in advanced cases.

myopia.name: Myopia (Nearsightedness)
myopia.description: Myopia is a refractive error causing distant objects to appear blurry while close objects can be seen clearly. High myopia can increase the risk of serious complications including retinal detachment, glaucoma, and macular degeneration.
symptoms: Blurry vision when looking at distant objects
Need to squint or partially close eyes
Difficulty seeing while driving at night
myopia.recommendation: Consult an optometrist or ophthalmologist for proper refraction testing. Corrective measures include glasses, contact lenses, or refractive surgery (LASIK/PRK). Regular follow-ups are important, especially for high myopia cases.
